function_descriptions: Catalyzes the peptide bond hydrolysis in dipeptides, displaying a non-redundant activity toward threonyl dipeptides (By similarity). Mediates threonyl dipeptide catabolism in a tissue- specific way (By similarity). Has high dipeptidase activity toward cysteinylglycine, an intermediate metabolite in glutathione metabolism (PubMed:12473676, PubMed:19346245). Metabolizes N-lactoyl-amino acids, both through hydrolysis to form lactic acid and amino acids, as well as through their formation by reverse proteolysis (PubMed:25964343). Plays a role in the regulation of cell cycle arrest and apoptosis (PubMed:17121880, PubMed:24395568). {ECO:0000250|UniProtKB:Q9D1A2, ECO:0000269|PubMed:12473676, ECO:0000269|PubMed:17121880, ECO:0000269|PubMed:19346245, ECO:0000269|PubMed:24395568, ECO:0000269|PubMed:25964343}.
